text: Literally, ‘harlot’, but it would appear from what follows, especially verse 14, that the warning here given is one against adultery, not fornication. It is uncertain, both here and elsewhere in the book, how much the Hebrew term ‘strange woman’ is meant to convey the sense of foreign birth.
Literally, ‘Let thy fountains be carried out in channels abroad; distribute thy well-water through the streets’; but the phrase evidently refers to a numerous family born in lawful wedlock.
